title: Why $ref resolution at seed time, not request time?

blocks.body: Resolving $ref pointers at seed time keeps request handling fast and avoids filesystem access during web requests. The Flask API serves pre-resolved JSON directly from the JsonDatabase. The tradeoff is that you must re-seed after content changes -- but since seeding takes under a second for 30+ sections, this is negligible. In production, the Docker entrypoint re-seeds automatically on every container start.
